Title: Gibberellin and Jasmonate Crosstalk during Stamen Development

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; pericarp tissues of developing reproductive organs
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for stamen filament elongation, anther dehiscence, pollen viability, and male fertility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-deficient mutant background used repeatedly in functional assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OPR3 causes JA deficiency and male sterility with short stamens; MYB21 overexpression can rescue this phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype; hormone application; overexpression rescue in mutant background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. opr3 is a JA-deficient mutant showing defective stamen filament elongation and male sterility. 2. GA cannot rescue MYB expression in opr3, indicating JA acts downstream of GA for this branch. 3. MYB21 overexpression restores fertility to opr3 flowers, placing OPR3 upstream of MYB-mediated stamen development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OPR3 is a JA-biosynthetic enzyme, in this review it mainly serves as a genetic background for JA deficiency rather than the primary focus of mechanistic regulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA signaling required for stamen elongation, anther dehiscence, pollen viability, and fertility
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JA signaling mutant coi1 exhibits male sterility and short stamens
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COI1 is the JA receptor-associated F-box protein in canonical JA signaling. 2. coi1 mutants display defective stamen maturation and male sterility. 3. COI1 acts upstream of JA-responsive transcriptional programs including MYB genes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/core summarization gene in this review for JA signaling, but not experimentally dissected here.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAZ degradation enables transcriptional activation of JA-responsive genes including some transcription factors
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cited signaling studies summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ proteins are repressors in JA signaling. 2. JA-Ile promotes COI1-JAZ binding and ubiquitin-dependent JAZ degradation. 3. Loss of JAZ repression activates JA-responsive genes relevant to stamen development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as common signaling component summarized in review; not directly tested in the focal experiments.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative GA-signaling regulator; transgenic expression retards stamen elongation and can induce male sterility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAI is a DELLA repressor of GA responses. 2. Persistent GAI activity retards stamen elongation and causes sterility. 3. Compared with RGA/RGL2, GAI is less effective in repressing MYB21/24/57 expression in reviewed genetic comparisons.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of DELLA family; not emphasized as one of the strongest repressors in this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative GA-signaling regulator contributing with other DELLAs to repression of floral development
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant combination studies cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL1 contributes with RGA and RGL2 to repression of petal and stamen development. 2. GA promotes floral development by triggering degradation of DELLAs including RGL1. 3. RGL1 is less effective than RGA/RGL2 in repressing MYB21/24/57 expression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a DELLA family member relevant to the reviewed network.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports general growth and normal floral organ development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation in GA1 causes GA deficiency, retarded floral development, short stamens, failed anthesis, and male sterility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA1 encodes an early GA-biosynthetic enzyme and ga1-3 is severely GA deficient. 2. ga1-3 flowers have arrested stamen filament elongation and defective anthesis. 3. Exogenous GA restores fertility, establishing the requirement of GA for stamen development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because ga1-3 is central genetic material throughout the reviewed studies, though GA1 itself is not the nutrient-metabolism focal output gene.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Phospholipase A1 DEFECTIVE IN ANTHER DEHISCENCE1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Releases α-linolenic acid from chloroplast membrane lipids to supply the initial substrate for jasmonate biosynthesis in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; young flower buds; developing reproductive organs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes normal stamen filament elongation, anther dehiscence, anthesis, and male fertility
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Greatly downregulated in ga1-3 and ga1-3 gai-t6 rga-t2 rgl1-1, partially restored in DELLA-deficient penta mutant; induced before MYB genes after GA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonate production required for normal stamen development; reduced DAD1 expression is associated with low JA and short stamens
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control step for JA production in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA; GA-deficient and DELLA-mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA upregulates DAD1 expression during stamen development. 2. DAD1 acts at an early step limiting initial substrate supply for JA biosynthesis in flowers. 3. Reduced DAD1 expression in GA-deficient mutants correlates with reduced JA levels and impaired stamen filament growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; gene expression analysis; hormone treatment; JA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes prior studies; DAD1 is highlighted as a major JA-biosynthetic gene under GA–DELLA control. AGAMOUS was suggested in cited work as a possible direct regulator of DAD1, but this is presented as a future hypothesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxygenation of fatty-acid substrate in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes normal stamen development and fertility through JA production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in ga1-3 and restored to WT in DELLA-deficient penta mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports JA biosynthesis downstream of GA-mediated DELLA relief
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key biosynthetic gene, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX1 expression is positively regulated by GA during floral development. 2. LOX1 expression is reduced in ga1-3 and restored when DELLAs are removed. 3. LOX1 likely contributes with DAD1 to GA-promoted JA production for stamen filament growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression analysis in hormone-related mutants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other JA biosynthetic genes were surveyed, but the review specifically concludes DAD1 and LOX1 are upregulated by GA.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive transcriptional control of stamen filament growth downstream of GA–JA crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for stamen filament elongation, anthesis, and fertility
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Stamen-enriched; low in young ga1-3 flower buds and restored in DELLA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-inducible in stamens/flower buds; induced by exogenous JA and by GA indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes stamen filament elongation and fertility; predominant among the three MYB genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential downstream effector of JA signaling and major determinant of normal stamen filament growth
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate; GA via DELLA repression relief and promotion of JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as a JA-response gene; expression repressed by DELLAs and induced by JA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DELLA proteins function upstream genetically; COI1-JAZ pathway acts upstream in JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous JA; exogenous GA; GA-deficient mutant; JA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB21 is a DELLA-repressed, stamen-enriched GA-response gene and also a JA-response gene. 2. MYB21 is the predominant MYB controlling stamen filament elongation, while MYB24 and MYB57 act redundantly with it. 3. Overexpression of MYB21 restores stamen filament elongation and fertility in opr3, placing it downstream of JA.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray/transcript profiling; semiquantitative RT-PCR; single/double/triple mutant analysis; epistasis analysis; overexpression rescue
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for engineering fertility or stamen development, but MYB21 alone is not sufficient to replace the full GA pathway in floral development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exogenous JA cannot rescue myb21 or myb21 myb24 short-stamen phenotype, indicating MYB21 acts downstream of JA.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive transcriptional control of stamen filament growth downstream of GA–JA crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for normal stamen development and fertility
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Stamen-enriched; low in ga1-3 young flower buds and restored in DELLA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA-inducible; induced by exogenous JA and indirectly by GA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with MYB21 to promote stamen filament elongation and fertility
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important but partially redundant JA-responsive effector for stamen maturation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate; GA via DELLA repression relief and promotion of JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as a JA-response gene; expression repressed by DELLAs and induced by JA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DELLA proteins function upstream genetically; COI1-JAZ pathway acts upstream in JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous JA; exogenous GA; GA-deficient mutant; JA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB24 is both a DELLA-repressed GA-response gene and a JA-response gene. 2. MYB24 functions redundantly with MYB21 in controlling stamen filament elongation. 3. myb21 myb24 mutations are epistatic to DELLA-deficient rescue backgrounds, confirming downstream action.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray/transcript profiling; semiquantitative RT-PCR; double-mutant and epistasis analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with MYB21 for manipulating stamen development, but redundancy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB24 alone is less dominant than MYB21 in the reviewed studies.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB family, closely related to subgroup 19 MYBs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive transcriptional control of stamen filament growth downstream of GA–JA crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to normal stamen filament growth and fertility
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Stamen-enriched; low in ga1-3 young flower buds and restored in DELLA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by exogenous JA; induced indirectly by GA through JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with MYB21/MYB24 in promoting stamen filament growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive downstream JA-responsive regulator required with MYB21/MYB24 for full stamen development
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate; GA via DELLA repression relief and promotion of JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as a JA-induced gene; expression repressed by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous JA; exogenous GA; GA-deficient mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB57 is a DELLA-repressed, stamen-enriched gene induced by JA. 2. MYB57 acts redundantly with MYB21 and MYB24 during stamen filament growth. 3. These MYB genes are necessary but not sufficient for the full GA-mediated floral developmental program.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; semiquantitative RT-PCR; mutant analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive target in multigene engineering of reproductive development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less extensively characterized than MYB21 and MYB24 in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA signaling control over JA biosynthetic gene expression and downstream stamen developmental transcription
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal stamen filament elongation and fertility when DELLA repression is relieved by GA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator; represses MYB21/MYB24/MYB57 expression and floral development until degraded by GA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the more effective DELLAs repressing the stamen-development transcriptional program
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB21; MYB24; MYB57; DAD1 (indirectly, as concluded in review)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses target gene expression until GA triggers DELLA degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via DELLA-mediated GA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: 26S proteasome-mediated degradation in response to GA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGA is a major DELLA repressor of floral development in Arabidopsis. 2. RGA is more effective than some other DELLAs in repressing MYB21, MYB24, and MYB57 in young flower buds. 3. GA promotes stamen development partly by triggering degradation of RGA.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic suppression analysis; mutant combinations; expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: DELLA loss can broadly alter GA responses beyond stamens
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating DELLA activity could modify fertility and floral development, but pleiotropic GA effects are likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review frames RGA as upstream regulator rather than terminal determinant of nutrient metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA signaling control over JA biosynthetic gene expression and downstream stamen developmental transcription
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal stamen filament elongation and fertility when DELLA repression is relieved by GA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein level not altered by JA treatment in discussed experiments
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator; strongly represses MYB21/MYB24/MYB57 and contributes to low JA-related developmental output in GA-deficient flowers
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the more effective DELLAs repressing expression of JA-responsive MYB genes during stamen development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB21; MYB24; MYB57; DAD1 (indirectly, as concluded in review)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses target gene expression until GA-triggered degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via DELLA-mediated GA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: 26S proteasome-mediated degradation in response to GA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA deficiency; exogenous GA; JA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL2 is a major DELLA repressor of floral development. 2. RGL2, together with RGA, most effectively represses MYB21, MYB24, and MYB57. 3. JA does not appear to act by altering RGL2 protein abundance in the reviewed experiments.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant combinations; gene expression analysis; hormone treatment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: DELLA perturbation has pleiotropic GA-signaling effects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream regulatory target, though broad developmental side effects are likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review centers on GA–DELLA regulation of JA pathway output during stamen development.
